title: AIDP Migrator Acceptance Contract
description: Optional per-notebook contract that asserts a streaming/batch pipeline drained successfully BEFORE the migrator marks the notebook PASS. Pattern adapted from a prior internal acceptance-contract pattern.

pending_count_sql.description: Single SQL statement that returns a numeric scalar = the count of pending/unfinished work items. Must converge to 0 for the contract to pass. Example: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM example_schema.file_state WHERE state IN ('PENDING_SILVER','PROCESSING')

zero_window.description: Required consecutive zero readings before the contract is declared PASSED. Use >=2 for streaming pipelines where a single zero between micro-batches is a false positive.

sleep_between_s.description: Seconds to wait between probe attempts. Set to 0 for batch pipelines where the answer should be available immediately.

max_attempts.description: Maximum probe attempts before giving up and marking the notebook VIOLATED. Total max wait time = max_attempts * sleep_between_s.

examples.description: Streaming silver-layer ingestion: require 2 consecutive zero readings, retry 10x with 30s between
examples.pending_count_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM example_schema.file_state WHERE state IN ('PENDING_SILVER','PROCESSING')

examples.description: Nightly batch ETL: assert target table has expected row count (single check, no retry)
examples.pending_count_sql: SELECT (1000 - COUNT(*)) AS pending FROM gold.daily_aggregates WHERE run_date = current_date()
